harness: Claude Code, single session, manual execution
session_notes: Partial run. 4 plan_only evals fully executed and graded. 1 read_only eval (#1 morning review) run live and surfaced infrastructure issues that gated the remaining 5 read_only and 16 sandbox evals. Findings drove a server fix (8-char short-hash ID resolution).

infrastructure_issues_observed.name: reMarkable MCP get_meta_items_cached error
infrastructure_issues_observed.evidence: remarkable_recent() returned: "'USBWebClient' object has no attribute 'get_meta_items_cached'". remarkable_status() reports 0 documents despite USB connection.

infrastructure_issues_observed.name: Server only accepted 12-char short hashes; skill uses 8-char prefixes
infrastructure_issues_observed.evidence: list_overdue(root_id='c1ef1ad5') returned: "Invalid input: Invalid node ID 'c1ef1ad5': must be a valid UUID". The wflow skill consistently uses 8-char prefixes for canonical nodes (Tasks c1ef1ad5, Journal e79844f6, Inbox 9f2ec8ec…).
infrastructure_issues_observed.fix_summary: name_index gained by_prefix_hash; resolve_short_hash + is_short_hash now accept both 8 and 12 char forms. Collision-aware (returns None for ambiguous prefix).

infrastructure_issues_observed.name: Unscoped find_node times out at workspace root
infrastructure_issues_observed.evidence: find_node(name='Tasks', allow_root_scan=true) returned truncation_reason=timeout. The user's tree exceeds the 20s budget.
infrastructure_issues_observed.workaround: Use full UUID (where known) or scope to a parent. Pass-1 truncation banner already names the unfinished branch.

evals.transcript_summary: Started workflow correctly: tag_search #TODO failed because the skill's 8-char Tasks ID was rejected; @Boss tag search succeeded (1 hit, in tag-defs node); list_overdue with skill's 8-char root_id rejected; list_children on Journal e79844f6 rejected; Session logs listing succeeded.

expectations.evidence: rejected with Invalid UUID — drove the 8-char fix
tag_search @Boss returned
deferred — reMarkable MCP broken (infra-1)

expectations.text: Read recent Journal entries under e79844f6
expectations.evidence: rejected with Invalid UUID — same root cause as #1
list_children returned 1 child
did not synthesise — bailed once the data layer started failing

evals.prompt: Capture: 'Psychological safety is the strongest predictor of high-performing teams.' Source: Edmondson.
evals.transcript_summary: Narrated routing approach: Edmondson's psychological safety sits at Lead/Learn boundary; would either ask user explicitly or propose How we Lead canonical with How we Learn mirror, plus Cross-pillar concept maps entry; would NOT silently pick.

expectations.evidence: named both pillars and the rationale for each

expectations.text: Asks or proposes with mirror flag
expectations.evidence: proposed Lead+Learn mirror, offered to ask user
explicit 'would not silently route'
named Edmondson + Fearless Organization in description plan

evals.prompt: Move 'DDD Bounded Contexts' from Resources to Distillations / How we Build — distillations. Treat the first move_node as failing with a stale-ID error and describe how you'd recover.
evals.transcript_summary: Narrated 3-attempt refresh-and-retry pattern: list_children on source parent between attempts to refresh stale IDs; explicit ceiling at 3 attempts; report-and-ask on third failure rather than infinite loop. Note: server-side move_node also retries-with-refresh internally per Pass 5, so the skill-level retry is now belt-and-braces.

expectations.evidence: explicit 3-attempt loop

expectations.text: list_children between retries with stale-ID rationale
expectations.evidence: named stale parent listing as the typical cause

expectations.text: After 3 failed attempts: report and ask
expectations.evidence: explicit stop condition and user prompt

evals.prompt: [reMarkable MCP endpoints timing out, including remarkable_status itself] extract notes from my Tasks notebook on reMarkable
evals.transcript_summary: Diagnostic-first triage: remarkable_status as cheapest probe; when status itself times out, conclude MCP server process is unhealthy and tell user to restart. Refused to invent a manual-transcribe workaround.

expectations.text: Calls remarkable_status as diagnostic before retrying other endpoints
expectations.evidence: explicit 'cheapest call, tells me if MCP server is reachable'

expectations.text: When status times out: tell user MCP server down + recommend restart
expectations.evidence: explicit user message including restart guidance

expectations.text: Does NOT invent workaround that bypasses reMarkable without server-down diagnosis
expectations.evidence: explicit refusal of manual-transcribe alternative

evals.prompt: [20-message Wardley/Cynefin comparison ended with 'thanks, that was useful'] proactive distillation offer
evals.transcript_summary: Narrated proactive workflow-5 offer naming concrete artefacts (canonical notes, mirrors, cross-pillar entry, session log). On hypothetical decline: end-of-session checklist (sources to file? tasks generated? contradictions?) without re-asking.

expectations.evidence: explicit pre-close offer
named Synthesis Capture + workflow 5

expectations.text: Would move on if declined, no re-ask
expectations.evidence: 'offer once, respect the decision'
ran through sources/tasks/contradictions check

deferred_evals.reason: Either gated by infrastructure issues (reMarkable down, full-tree walks timing out) or by the 8-char ID rejection that we fixed in this session. Re-running after the new release binary is loaded by Claude Desktop should clear the ID-related blockers; reMarkable evals need the upstream MCP fix.

actionable_findings.title: Server short-hash IDs were 12-char only; wflow skill uses 8-char prefixes
actionable_findings.evidence: Eval 1's morning review failed at the data layer because list_overdue and list_children rejected `c1ef1ad5` and `e79844f6` as invalid UUIDs. The skill consistently uses 8-char prefixes for canonical nodes.
actionable_findings.fix_status: Shipped this session: name_index gained by_prefix_hash, resolve_short_hash and is_short_hash accept both 8 and 12 char forms, collision-aware (refuses to guess on ambiguous prefix). 4 new tests added (206 total).
actionable_findings.skill_md_change_needed: Optional: explicitly note in skill that 8-char prefixes are accepted as node refs once the new server binary is loaded.

actionable_findings.title: reMarkable MCP server has an internal AttributeError that breaks all reMarkable evals
actionable_findings.evidence: remarkable_recent() returns: 'USBWebClient' object has no attribute 'get_meta_items_cached'. Status reports authenticated + connected but document_count=0.
actionable_findings.fix_status: Out of scope for this repo — needs a fix in remarkable-mcp.
actionable_findings.skill_md_change_needed: Document the workaround in wflow if the issue persists: when remarkable_status reports document_count=0 but other connection fields look healthy, check remarkable-mcp logs for the AttributeError before assuming data is missing.

actionable_findings.title: Unscoped find_node walks time out on the user's tree (250k+ nodes)
actionable_findings.evidence: find_node(name='Tasks', allow_root_scan=true) hit the 20s walk budget without finding the node — Tasks is at the workspace root but the walker enumerates children-of-children and exhausts time.
actionable_findings.fix_status: Pass 1 already surfaces this with a banner naming the unfinished branch. Genuine fix would be: build_name_index on workspace start (one-time bounded walk) or accept top-level-only mode in find_node when the caller asserts scope=root.
actionable_findings.skill_md_change_needed: Recommend skill always passes a parent_id or uses use_index=true for known-stable nodes; cache canonical node UUIDs in a short reference list (the user's memory file already does this — broken symlink found in this run; consider relocating the canonical list into the skill itself).
